mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals an easing monetary policy stance that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive returns following easing periods are not statistically distinguishable from unconditional base rate (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window, it signals an accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes broad earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical instances where the UNRATE trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive, or Rank IC conditional on trigger activation is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) reflects simultaneous contraction in construction and consumer demand, a combined shock that is more predictive of an equity drawdown over a quarterly horizon than either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST YoY decline > 20% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1%
failure_condition: The joint HOUST-RSAFS trigger fires but 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction model's Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series model's IC by at least 0.02 across validation folds.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output, which is positively associated with near-term S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as it signals continued operational momentum.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly change and 5-day forward SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips sign persistently for more than 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: During a systemic credit crisis, an emergency FEDFUNDS rate cut of more than 50 basis points in a single action signals policymaker recognition of acute financial stress; in this regime the cut fails to restore confidence immediately and forward S&P 500 returns over 20 days are negative as markets price continued deleveraging, contrary to the normal easing-is-bullish relationship.
evidence_refs: fact:large emergency rate cuts historically coincide with acute banking-system stress events
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns following large emergency cuts are positive in more than 55% of instances during crisis-labeled periods, invalidating the regime-conditional sign flip.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month average decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding -100,000 jobs signals a broadening employment contraction that leads corporate earnings estimates lower with a lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly (60-day) horizon.
failure_condition: Following trigger activation, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is not robust to PAYEMS benchmark revisions exceeding 50000 jobs.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) respond to federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) cuts with a lag; when FEDFUNDS is falling while HOUST is simultaneously still contracting, the market anticipates future stabilization in residential construction, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as this joint pattern precedes housing-sector-led recovery expectations.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines for 3+ consecutive months while HOUST YoY remains negative
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-HOUST trigger fires but 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over the FEDFUNDS-only trend model.

mechanism_narrative: A stagflation-like interaction where CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates month-over-month while INDPRO growth simultaneously decelerates signals eroding real corporate margins under rising input costs without offsetting output growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM change > 0.3% AND INDPRO MoM change < 0%
failure_condition: The joint CPI-INDPRO trigger fires but 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the interaction model fails to outperform independent single-series baselines by at least 0.02 Rank IC.
